ntistools 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • Cleaning NTIS Data: Before and After ntistools

CLAUDE.md

Source: CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

Project Overview

ntistools is an R package for wrangling Nonprofit Trends and Impacts Survey (NTIS) data, developed by the Urban Institute. It provides reusable data-cleaning functions that replace repetitive dplyr::case_when() / dplyr::across() patterns common in NTIS analysis scripts. Internal use only; contains no raw survey data or PII.

Common Commands

# Run all tests
Rscript -e 'testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat")'

# Run a single test file
Rscript -e 'testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-recode_binary.R")'

# Check package (runs R CMD check)
Rscript -e 'devtools::check()'

# Rebuild documentation (roxygen2 -> man/ and NAMESPACE)
Rscript -e 'devtools::document()'

# Install package locally
Rscript -e 'devtools::install()'

# Build vignettes
Rscript -e 'devtools::build_vignettes()'

Architecture

Standard R package layout using devtools/roxygen2 workflow:

  • R/: All exported functions, one per file. Each function takes a data frame as first argument and returns the modified data frame (pipe-friendly).
  • man/: Auto-generated from roxygen2 comments in R/ files. Never edit directly.
  • NAMESPACE: Auto-generated by roxygen2. Never edit directly.
  • tests/testthat/: One test file per function (test-{function_name}.R). Uses testthat edition 3.
  • vignettes/: before-and-after.Rmd shows how each function replaces raw dplyr code.
  • inst/examples/: sample_data_cleaning_script.R is a real-world NTIS cleaning script showing the patterns these functions replace.

Key Conventions

  • Dependencies: Only dplyr and rlang. Keep it minimal.
  • Column selection: Functions accept column names as character vectors via vars parameter (used with dplyr::all_of()). Exception: combine_binary and count_binary use ... with unquoted (tidyselect) names.
  • Survey sentinel values: NTIS uses numeric sentinels — 98 = “don’t know”, 97 = “not applicable”, 0 = “none”. Many functions have defaults reflecting these conventions.
  • NA propagation pattern: When all source columns are NA, derived columns should also be NA (not 0). This is a deliberate design choice used across combine_binary, count_binary, and apply_filter.
  • roxygen2 with markdown: Enabled via Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE) in DESCRIPTION. Use markdown syntax in roxygen comments.
  • rlang imports: .data and := are imported from rlang in R/ntistools-package.R for tidy evaluation.

On this page

Developed by Thiyaghessan Poongundranar.
